summary: Iteration 5: full E2E validation of header/Settings/language refactor. Backend pytest 12/12 (TestAuth incl. PATCH /me lang) PASS. Frontend Playwright covered 9 review criteria — 17/18 sub-checks PASS; the only failed check (catechism coming-soon body text on screen) is a non-bug because the ES Catechism dataset now exists, so the conditional coming-soon banner no longer renders; both Spanish and English translation strings are correct at source (LangContext.jsx lines 223 + 604).
critical_code_review_comments: LangContext.jsx — adoption guard via adoptedForUserId.useRef(user.id) correctly fires only on the first user-resolution event per session, so a logged-in toggle isn't clobbered by a stale /me. Verified: localStorage='es' was overridden to 'en' on login when server.lang='en'.

LangContext setLang() PATCH /auth/me is fire-and-forget with .catch() swallowing failures. Acceptable: anonymous fallback already saved locally; however a transient network error would leave UI lang and server lang out of sync until next manual toggle. Consider toast on failure for logged-in users.

AuthContext.register() does NOT pass `lang` to backend (only email/password/name). Default 'es' is set server-side which matches spec, but if you ever expose a lang chooser at registration time you'll need to thread it through. Backend already accepts data.lang on /register.

Settings.jsx settings-language section: the buttons rely solely on aria-checked + visual styling — no focus ring is overridden, default tailwind focus-visible still works. radiogroup role + role='radio' on buttons is correct.

Catechism.jsx coming-soon Spanish banner appears to be dead code now that ES dataset is shipped (per iteration_4). String change to 'desde Ajustes' is preserved in LangContext but won't render in production. Either remove the dead branch or keep as defensive fallback — current behaviour is safe.

Layout.jsx header right-side cluster: 3 icon-only buttons (Heart / Gear / Login-Logout). All three carry data-testid + aria-label + title. NavLink active state uses different colors per icon (sangre for favorites, purple for settings) — consistent with existing UX language.

success_rate.frontend: 94% (17/18 sub-checks; 1 N/A due to dead coming-soon branch)

test_credentials: admin@apostol.app / Apostol2026! (works). Throwaway users created via /api/auth/register during test.
seed_data_creation: Two throwaway users created via /api/auth/register (TEST-prefixed-uuid emails, will be ignored by future cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Header now exposes data-testid='header-favorites-link' (Heart icon) and data-testid='header-settings-link' (Gear icon). lang-toggle/lang-toggle-es/lang-toggle-en NO LONGER EXIST anywhere — selecting them will fail. Language is changed exclusively from /settings via data-testid='settings-language-es' / 'settings-language-en' (aria-checked='true' on active). Anonymous-only hint at data-testid='settings-language-anonymous-hint'. Backend: PATCH /api/auth/me accepts {lang: 'es'|'en'}; rejects other values with 400; requires auth (401 otherwise). On login the React app adopts user.lang from /auth/me and overrides any anonymous localStorage 'apostol_lang'. The user object now ALWAYS carries `lang` (defaults to 'es' for legacy users without the field, see auth.py _user_public). Sidebar 'nav-favorites' and 'more-nav-favorites' testids no longer exist — favorites is reachable only via header-favorites-link or direct /favorites URL.
